scenario: A team's Tasklist has assignees in Bulgaria, Germany, and Japan. They wonder about i18n / localisation -- can forms show labels in each user's preferred language?
question: Localisation / i18n for Camunda Forms?

options.text: Camunda Forms automatically translates all field labels, descriptions, and placeholder text into each user's browser language using built-in auto-translation capabilities. This misrepresents the documented behavior. Camunda Forms does not include automatic translation. Form content -- field labels, descriptions, help texts -- is authored in the language chosen by the modeller at design time. There is no native browser-locale detection that automatically switches form label language. Providing multi-language forms requires deliberate design patterns such as deploying separate forms per locale or using a custom front-end rendering layer.

The Camunda Tasklist interface and all form content are permanently in English only, with no support for displaying the UI in other languages. This is inaccurate. The Camunda Tasklist UI chrome -- navigation menus, buttons, and system messages -- supports multiple languages depending on the Camunda 8 version. Tasklist itself may support German, French, and other languages beyond English, depending on the release.

) for the chrome (menus, buttons). Form content (field labels, descriptions, help text) is typically authored once per form deployment; native per-user language switching within a single form depends on Forms support in the specific Camunda version -- check current capabilities. Workarounds: deploy separate forms per locale and route by user preference variable; use dynamic label expressions if FEEL templating supports locale lookups; or build a custom front-end with a full i18n stack (React + react-i18next) that renders task data fetched via the Tasklist API.

Camunda 8 Tasklist and Forms have zero internationalisation support; the product is designed exclusively for English-language deployments and has no i18n architecture. This misrepresents the documented behavior. Camunda 8 ships Tasklist with UI chrome localisation for multiple languages. The platform also provides patterns for multi-language form content, including deploying locale-specific forms, using FEEL-based dynamic content, or integrating custom front-ends. Camunda's documentation and community resources address i18n as a supported design concern.

a.text: Incorrect. Camunda Forms / Tasklist i18n landscape: Tasklist UI chrome: Standard UI elements (menus, navigation, buttons) typically have multi-language support. User preference via Tasklist settings. Languages supported: depends on Camunda 8 version -- typically English, German, French, etc. Form content (the form fields themselves): Authored once per form: field labels, descriptions, help texts in the language the modeler chose. Native per-user switching: not always a primary feature in standard Camunda Forms; verify current version. Workarounds for true per-user form i18n: Pattern 1: Multiple forms per language: Form "review-form-en" (English labels). Form "review-form-bg" (Bulgarian labels). Form "review-form-de" (German labels). At Service Task before User Task: set formId based on user's preferred language. Pros: simple, native; cons: maintenance overhead (n × forms to keep in sync). Pattern 2: Field-label keys + lookup: Form labels are static keys (e.g., field.customerName.label). JavaScript-side: lookup translations from a dictionary based on user locale. Requires Tasklist customisation; not vanilla. Pattern 3: External rendering: Custom front-end fetches task data via Tasklist API. Renders fields with own i18n stack (React + react-i18next, etc.). Full control; significant build. Pattern 4: Server-side localisation in form: If forms support dynamic labels via FEEL / template expressions: Use process variable userLocale to render appropriate labels. Verify support per Forms version. For data values (not labels): Localised content stored in localized resources (DB tables by locale, etc.). Process variables look up per user. Best practices: Decouple labels from data: labels = i18n concern (UI); data = process concern (engine). Engineering vs business trade-off: native + multi-form simplest, customisation most flexible. Verify exact form i18n capabilities per Camunda 8 / Forms version.
